title: Withdraw 120,000,000 ada for AlphaGrowth’s Cardano PRIME

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90M in TVL and $45M in stablecoin supply." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Cardano PRIME is a 12-month program intended to improve DeFi protocol readiness, activate incentives responsibly, and grow durable liquidity across Cardano markets." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90M in TVL and $45M in stablecoin supply; Provide independent evidence for: AlphaGrowth has not received Cardano Treasury funding within the prior 24 months. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.11; confidence 77.8%.
rationale.summary_raw: Directional NO forced: the applicable Net Change Limit is exhausted — treasury withdrawals already enacted within this NCL period leave no remaining capacity.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90M in TVL and $45M in stablecoin supply.
Provide independent evidence for: AlphaGrowth has not received Cardano Treasury funding within the prior 24 months.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund Cardano PRIME, a 12-month community-overseen program to improve DeFi protocol readiness, activate incentives, and grow durable liquidity across Cardano markets.

Recipient: Intersect treasury reserve stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, with execution by AlphaGrowth under Operating Group oversight and Intersect fund management.

Deliverables: Phase 1 public current-state audit, Phase 2 identification of integration, product, and ecosystem-support gaps, Phase 3 incentives and capital deployment after Operating Group affirmation, Published recommendations and disbursement records, Quarterly financial and ecosystem impact reporting, Independent audit or assurance funded by a dedicated 2000000 ada allocation, Return-to-treasury handling for unused, unearned, unreleased, or excess funds

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Cardano PRIME is a 12-month program intended to improve DeFi protocol readiness, activate incentives responsibly, and grow durable liquidity across Cardano markets.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The withdrawal requests 120000000 ada, with USD planning figures based on a $0.16 per ADA assumption.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Program funds are to be managed directly by Intersect in a separate auditable account rather than unilaterally held or controlled by AlphaGrowth.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal makes the withdrawal conditional on an applicable Net Change Limit under TREASURY-01a having enough remaining capacity to cover the withdrawal in full at enactment.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90M in TVL and $45M in stablecoin supply.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The program uses a phased model in which Phase 3 incentives and capital are deployed only after the Operating Group affirms the Phase 3 release plan.
missing: Independent evidence for: As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90M in TVL and $45M in stablecoin supply.
Independent evidence for: AlphaGrowth has not received Cardano Treasury funding within the prior 24 months.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Cardano PRIME is a 12-month program intended to improve DeFi protocol readiness, activate incentives responsibly, and grow durable liquidity across Cardano markets." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "As of June 2026, Cardano DeFi has approximately $90M in TVL and $45M in stablecoin supply." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
affirmative_waste_evidence: independently verified excessive cost versus market comparables
missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity
cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, whose voting CLOSED with a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.

pending_candidate.note: Voting on this action is still open; its interim tally does not set the operative ceiling. It becomes the pin only if it closes with Yes > No.
